root_cause: The OCS boundary contract existed without an executable runtime capable of assembling deterministic replay-visible context evidence.
runtime_model: OCS context assembly consumes explicit source context grouped by conversation, clarification, PPP, approval, replay-visible operation, domain, and registry categories.
Each accepted source must be replay-visible.
Source artifact hashes are verified when present.
Sources are normalized into deterministic ids, artifact types, source hashes, and summaries.
Duplicate source fingerprints are suppressed and recorded.
The assembled context records empty sections explicitly.
Context hash is derived from normalized inputs, rejected duplicate records, context sections, known gaps, authority flags, and status.
Replay reconstruction validates ordering, wrapper hashes, artifact hashes, returned continuity, and context hash continuity.
replay_impact: OCS context assembly now produces append-only replay-visible evidence.
Replay reconstruction can prove what OCS saw and what it did not accept.
Repeated deterministic source inputs produce the same context hash.
The runtime does not mutate source replay.
The runtime does not depend on provider, worker, dispatch, or execution side effects.
boundaries_preserved: No replay mutation outside append-only OCS evidence.
